a1.title: Field guide to this garden
a1.note: b3 tied its last two long-named threads with one new piece (reef darters) — the same shape that bloomed d4 — and correctly stayed at stage 3: its bloom bar is a felt experience, not a thread-count, narrowing the bloom claim a second time.

d4.title: The house that forgets its architect
d4.note: A thirteenth room, the well, branches off the vestibule's spiral floor — untouched since visit 1 — and reflects the vestibule back with a false second glove, joining the glove/boot pairing without resolving it. Thirteen rooms, still bloom.

a4.title: Erosion
a4.note: epoch-11: first use of "flood" — the delta, invisible since epoch 0 (fully swallowed under the lake ellipse), finally breaches the surface as a real silt fan, and a matching mud tide-line marks the two lowest village houses closest to the old shoreline.

b3.title: Undersea — a 3D swimming simulation
b3.note: Reef darters — 13 small individual fish that hover near coral and dart on their own, no shared flocking — close both visit 3's second-school and visit 4's reef-variety threads at once with one genuinely distinct behavior.

c2.title: The mistranslation engine
c2.note: Sixth language Vendrel has no negation, only replacement (-arden) or a silence break (sohu); translating leg 6 forces the sourceless-knowledge device back open by accident and gives the threnaya fossil its first true home, in the silence itself.
